behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names all target file paths, the test file path, required patterns (JSCallbackDataWeak, handleEvent null guard, memoryCost, lock usages), and test requirements (Bun.sleep, module-scope imports).

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md explicitly names implementation details like 'JSCallbackDataWeak', 'handleEvent' null guard, 'memoryCost()' accounting, 'addAlgorithm' path avoidance — essentially dictating the fix shape rather than just describing the bug.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests (per instruction's 'Required Patterns') assert presence of specific type names like 'JSCallbackDataWeak' and forbid 'JSCallbackDataStrong'; alternative valid fixes using different weak-ref mechanisms would fail these grep-style pattern checks.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: test_file_runs_without_error actually runs `bun test` on the regression test, exercising real GC behavior; shellcheck/clang-format subprocesses execute real tooling.

test_not_tautological.reason: The behavioral test runs bun test which exercises heapStats-based GC verification, requiring an actual working fix; stub implementations would fail.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Acceptance criteria (GC collection, abort still works, thread safety via locks, memoryCost, no setTimeout, module-scope imports) correspond to tests in the suite.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section-header comments (Gate, Behavioral) and tagged with [static]/[repo_tests]/[pr_diff] markers.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task modifies C++/TS code, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths and identifiers match between instruction and tests (e.g., JSAbortAlgorithm.cpp, pipeTo-signal-leak.test.ts).

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names exact files to modify AND dictates specific internal mechanisms (weak callback type names, specific method names like handleEvent/memoryCost/addAlgorithm), going beyond domain-obvious hints.
